title: Authenticated Encryption with AES-GCM · Execution Trace: Crypto Go Aad Binding
summary: A multi-tenant service reuses GCM nonces after restart, omits tenant and version context from additional data, and releases plaintext before authentication succeeds. Predict the asset, adversary, security property, trust boundary, and falsifying evidence before opening the governing standard. Planned artifact: Add and verify the aad binding increment in versioned AES-GCM record envelope. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence…
objectives: Bind protocol version, tenant, object identity, algorithm suite, direction, and other non-secret context as canonical associated data.
Design a crash-, concurrency-, rollback-, and replication-safe GCM nonce strategy within the construction limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a versioned AES-GCM envelope, nonce budget, AAD, lengths, and atomic plaintext release; record key custody, terminal failure, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk ownership; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bind protocol version, tenant, object identity, algorithm suite, direction, and other non-secret context as canonical associated data. A defensible solution applies Crypto Go Aad Binding, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to versioned AES-GCM record envelope.

content.code:
package main

import (
	"crypto/aes"
	"crypto/cipher"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	key := make([]byte, 16)
	key[0] = 4
	block, err := aes.NewCipher(key)
	if err != nil { panic(err) }
	aead, err := cipher.NewGCM(block)
	if err != nil { panic(err) }
	nonce := make([]byte, aead.NonceSize())
	nonce[len(nonce)-1] = 4
	aad := []byte("v1|tenant-a|record-4")
	ciphertext := aead.Seal(nil, nonce, []byte("private-evidence"), aad)
	plaintext, openErr := aead.Open(nil, nonce, ciphertext, aad)
	tampered := append([]byte(nil), ciphertext...)
	tampered[len(tampered)-1] ^= 1
	_, tamperErr := aead.Open(nil, nonce, tampered, aad)
	fmt.Printf("opened=%t plaintext=%s tamper_rejected=%t\n", openErr == nil, plaintext, tamperErr != nil)
}
// Worked focus: crypto-go-aad-binding — Bind protocol version, tenant, object identity, algorithm suite, direction, and other non-secret context as canonical associated data.
